claims.claim_text: California has provided a meager $200 to individuals upon their release from the California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation (CDCR) for post-release expenses related to transportation, clothing, and other essential expenses since 1973.
CDCR can deduct money from the release allowance to cover the costs of transportation and clothing.
Many individuals receive less than $120 to meet their basic needs in the critical first days after their release due to these deductions.
Senator Becker secured a $1.8 million investment to ensure that the full release allowance is given to individuals without any deductions.
This investment is expected to benefit approximately 10,000 individuals each year affected by these deductions.
Providing the full release allowance is vital for successful reentry and the overall well-being of communities.
